text: The word of the Lord, that came to Micheas, the Morasthite, in the days of Joathan, Achaz, and Ezechias, kings of Juda: which he saw concerning Samaria and Jerusalem.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Hear, all ye people: and let the earth give ear, and all that is therein: and let the Lord God be a witness to you, the Lord from his holy temple.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: For behold the Lord will come forth out of his place: and he will come down, and will tread upon the high places of the earth.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the mountains shall be melted under him: and the valleys shall be cleft, as wax before the fire, and as waters that run down a steep place.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: For the wickedness of Jacob is all this, and for the sins of the house of Israel. What is the wickedness of Jacob? is it not Samaria? and what are the high places of Juda? are they not Jerusalem?

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And I will make Samaria as a heap of stones in the field when a vineyard is planted: and I will bring down the stones thereof into the valley, and will lay her foundations bare.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And all her graven things shall be cut in pieces, and all her wages shall be burnt with fire, and I will bring to destruction all her idols: for they were gathered together of the hire of a harlot, and unto the hire of a harlot they shall return.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Therefore will I lament, and howl: I will go stript and naked: I will make a wailing like the dragons, and a mourning like the ostriches.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Because her wound is desperate, because it is come even to Juda, it hath touched the gate of my people, even to Jerusalem.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Declare ye it not in Geth, weep ye not with tears: in the house of Dust sprinkle yourselves with dust.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And pass away, O thou that dwellest in the beautiful place, covered with thy shame: she went not forth that dwelleth in the confines: the house adjoining shall receive mourning from you, which stood by herself.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: For she is become weak unto good that dwelleth in bitterness: for evil is come down from the Lord into the gate of Jerusalem.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: A tumult of chariots hath astonished the inhabitants of Lachis: it is the beginning of sin to the daughter of Sion for in thee were found the crimes of Israel.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Therefore shall she send messengers to the inheritance of Geth: the houses of lying to deceive the kings of Israel.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Yet will I bring an heir to thee that dwellest in Maresa: even to Odollam shall the glory of Israel come.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Make thee bald, and be polled for thy delicate children: enlarge thy baldness as the eagle: for they are carried into captivity from thee.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Woe to you that devise that which is unprofitable, and work evil in your beds: in the morning light they execute it, because their hand is against God.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And they have coveted fields, and taken them by violence, and houses they have forcibly taken away: and oppressed a man and his house, a man and his inheritance.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Therefore thus saith the Lord: Behold I devise an evil against this family: from which you shall not withdraw your necks, and you shall not walk haughtily, for this is a very evil time.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: In that day a parable shall be taken up upon you, and a song shall be sung with melody by them that say: We are laid waste and spoiled: the portion of my people is changed: how shall he depart from me, whereas he is returning that will divide our land?

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Therefore thou shalt have none that shall cast the cord of a lot in the assembly of the Lord.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Speak ye not, saying: It shall not drop upon these, confusion shall not take them.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The house of Jacob saith: Is the Spirit of the Lord straitened or are these his thoughts? Are not my words good to him that walketh uprightly?

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But my people, on the contrary, are risen up as an enemy: you have taken away the cloak off from the coat: and them that passed harmless you have turned to war.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: You have cast out the women of my people from their houses, in which they took delight: you have taken my praise forever from their children.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Arise ye, and depart, for there is no rest here for you. For that uncleanness of the land, it shall be corrupted with a grievous corruption.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Would God I were not a man that hath the spirit, and that I rather spoke a lie: I will let drop to thee of wine, and of drunkenness: and it shall be this people upon whom it shall drop.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: I will assemble and gather together all of thee, O Jacob: I will bring together the remnant of Israel, I will put them together as a flock in the fold, as sheep in the midst of the sheepcotes, they shall make a tumult by reason of the multitude of men.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: For he shall go up that shall open the way before them: they shall divide and pass through the gate, and shall come in by it: and their king shall pass before them, and the Lord at the head of them.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And I said: Hear, O ye princes of Jacob, and ye chiefs of the house of Israel: Is it not your part to know judgment,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: You that hate good, and love evil: that violently pluck off their skins from them and their flesh from their bones?

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Who have eaten the flesh of my people, and have flayed their skin off them: and have broken, and chopped their bones as for the kettle, and as flesh in the midst of the pot.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Then shall they cry to the Lord, and he will not hear them: and he will hide his face from them at that time, as they have behaved wickedly in their devices.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Thus saith the Lord concerning the prophets that make my people err: that bite with their teeth, and preach peace: and if a man give not something into their mouth, they prepare war against him.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Therefore night shall be to you instead of vision, and darkness to you instead of divination: and the sun shall go down upon the prophets, and the day shall be darkened over them.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And they shall be confounded that see visions, and the diviners shall be confounded: and they shall all cover their faces, because there is no answer of God.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But yet I am filled with the strength of the spirit of the Lord, with judgment and power: to declare unto Jacob his wickedness and to Israel his sin.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Hear this, ye princes of the house of Jacob, and ye judges of the house of Israel: you that abhor judgment and pervert all that is right.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: You that build up Sion with blood, and Jerusalem with iniquity.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Her princes have judged for bribes: and her priests have taught for hire, and her prophets divined for money: and they leaned upon the Lord, saying: Is not the Lord in the midst of us? no evil shall come upon us.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Therefore because of you, Sion shall be ploughed as a field, and Jerusalem shall be as a heap of stones, and the mountain of the temple as the high places of the forests.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And it shall come to pass in the last days, that the mountain of the house of the Lord shall be prepared in the top of the mountains, and high above the hills: and people shall flow to it.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And many nations shall come in haste, and say: Come, let us go up to the mountain of the Lord, and to the house of the God of Jacob: and he will teach us of his ways, and we will walk in his paths: for the law shall go forth out of Sion, and the word of the Lord out of Jerusalem.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And he shall judge among many people, and rebuke strong nations afar off: and they shall beat their swords into ploughshares, and their spears into spades: nation shall not take sword against nation: neither shall they learn war anymore.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And every man shall sit under his vine, and under his fig tree, and there shall be none to make them afraid, for the mouth of the Lord of hosts hath spoken.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: For all people will walk every one in the name of his god: but we will walk in the name of the Lord, our God, for ever and ever.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: In that day, saith the Lord, I will gather up her that halteth: and her that I had cast out, I will gather up: and her whom I had afflicted.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And I will make her that halted, a remnant: and her that had been afflicted, a mighty nation: and the Lord will reign over them in Mount Sion, from this time now and forever.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And thou, O cloudy tower of the flock, of the daughter of Sion, unto thee shall it come: yea the first power shall come, the kingdom to the daughter of Jerusalem.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Now, why art thou drawn together with grief? Hast thou no king in thee, or is thy counselor perished, because sorrow hath taken thee as a woman in labour.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Be in pain and labour, O daughter of Sion, as a woman that bringeth forth: for now shalt thou go out of the city, and shalt dwell in the country, and shalt come even to Babylon, there thou shalt be delivered: there the Lord will redeem thee out of the hand of thy enemies.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And now many nations are gathered together against thee, and they say: Let her be stoned: and let our eye look upon Sion.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But they have not known the thoughts of the Lord, and have not understood his counsel: because he hath gathered them together as the hay of the floor.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Arise, and tread, O daughter of Sion: for I will make thy horn iron, and thy hoofs I will make brass: and thou shalt beat in pieces many peoples, and shalt immolate the spoils of them to the Lord, and their strength to the Lord of the whole earth.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Now shalt thou be laid waste, O daughter of the robber: they have laid siege against us, with a rod shall they strike the cheek of the judge of Israel.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And thou Bethlehem Ephrata, art a little one among the thousands of Juda, out of thee shall he come forth unto me that is to be the ruler in Israel: and his going forth is from the beginning, from the days of eternity.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Therefore will he give them up even till the time wherein she that travaileth shall bring forth: and the remnant of his brethren shall be converted to the children of Israel.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And he shall stand, and feed in the strength of the Lord, in the height of the name of the Lord, his God: and they shall be converted, for now shall he be magnified even to the ends of the earth.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And this man shall be our peace, when the Assyrian shall come into our land, and when he shall set his foot in our houses: and we shall raise against him seven shepherds, and eight principal men.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And they shall feed the land of Assyria with the sword, and the land of Nemrod with the spears thereof: and he shall deliver us from the Assyrian when he shall come into our land, and when he shall tread in our borders.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the remnant of Jacob shall be in the midst of many peoples, as a dew from the Lord, and as drops upon the grass, which waiteth not for man, nor tarrieth for the children of men.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the remnant of Jacob shall be among the Gentiles, in the midst of many peoples, as a lion among the beasts of the forests, and as a young lion among the flocks of sheep: who, when he shall go through, and tread down, and take there is none to deliver.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Thy hand shall be lifted up over thy enemies, and all thy enemies shall be cut off.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And it shall come to pass in that day, saith the Lord, that I will take away thy horses out of the midst of thee, and will destroy thy chariots.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And I will destroy the cities of thy land, and will throw down all thy strong holds, and I will take away sorceries out of thy hand, and there shall be no divinations in thee.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And I will destroy thy graven things, and thy statues, out of the midst of thee: and thou shalt no more adore the works of thy hands.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And I will pluck up thy groves out of the midst of thee: and will crush thy cities.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And I will execute vengeance in wrath, and in indignation, among all the nations that have not given ear.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Hear ye what the Lord saith: Arise, contend thou in judgment against the mountains, and let the hills hear thy voice.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Let the mountains hear the judgment of the Lord, and the strong foundations of the earth: for the Lord will enter into judgment with his people, and he will plead against Israel.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: O my people, what have I done to thee, or in what have I molested thee? answer thou me.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: For I brought thee up out of the land of Egypt, and delivered thee out of the house of slaves: and I sent before thy face Moses, and Aaron, and Mary.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: O my people, remember, I pray thee, what Balach, the king of Moab, purposed: and what Balaam, the son of Beor, answered him, from Setim to Galgal, that thou mightest know the justice of the Lord.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: What shall I offer to the Lord that is worthy? wherewith shall I kneel before the high God? shall I offer holocausts unto him, and calves of a year old?

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: May the Lord be appeased with thousands of rams, or with many thousands of fat he goats? shall I give my firstborn for my wickedness, the fruit of my body for the sin of my soul?

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: I will shew thee, O man, what is good, and what the Lord requireth of thee: Verily to do judgment, and to love mercy, and to walk solicitous with thy God.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The voice of the Lord crieth to the city, and salvation shall be to them that fear thy name: hear O ye tribes, and who shall approve it?

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: As yet there is a fire in the house of the wicked, the treasures of iniquity, and a scant measure full of wrath.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Shall I justify wicked balances, and the deceitful weights of the bag?

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: By which her rich men were filled with iniquity, and the inhabitants thereof have spoken lies, and their tongue was deceitful in their mouth.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And I therefore began to strike thee with desolation for thy sins.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Thou shalt eat, but shalt not be filled: and thy humiliation shall be in the midst of thee: and thou shalt take hold, but shalt not save: and those whom thou shalt save, I will give up to the sword.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Thou shalt sow, but shalt not reap: thou shalt tread the olives, but shalt not be anointed with the oil: and the new wine, but shalt not drink the wine.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: For thou hast kept the statutes of Amri, and all the works of the house of Achab: and thou hast walked according to their wills, that I should make thee a desolation, and the inhabitants thereof a hissing, and you shall bear the reproach of my people.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Woe is me, for I am become as one that gleaneth in autumn the grapes of the vintage: there is no cluster to eat, my soul desired the first ripe figs.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The holy man is perished out of the earth, and there is none upright among men: they all lie in wait for blood, every one hunteth his brother to death.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The evil of their hands they call good: the prince requireth, and the judge is for giving: and the great man hath uttered the desire of his soul, and they have troubled it.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: He that is best among them, is as a brier, and he that is righteous, as the thorn of the hedge. The day of thy inspection, thy visitation cometh: now shall be their destruction.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Believe not a friend, and trust not in a prince: keep the doors of thy mouth from her that sleepeth in thy bosom.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: For the son dishonoureth the father, and the daughter riseth up against her mother, the daughter in law against her mother in law: and a man’s enemies are they of his own household.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But I will look towards the Lord, I will wait for God, my saviour: my God will hear me.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Rejoice not, thou my enemy, over me, because I am fallen: I shall arise, when I sit in darkness, the Lord is my light.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: I will bear the wrath of the Lord, because I have sinned against him: until he judge my cause, and execute judgment for me: he will bring me forth into the light, I shall behold his justice.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And my enemy shall behold, and she shall be covered with shame, who saith to me: Where is the Lord thy God? my eyes shall look down upon her: now shall she be trodden under foot as the mire of the streets.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The day shall come, that thy walls may be built up: in that day shall the law be far removed.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: In that day they shall come even from Assyria to thee, and to the fortified cities: and from the fortified cities even to the river, and from sea to sea, and from mountain to mountain.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the land shall be made desolate because of the inhabitants thereof, and for the fruit of their devices.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Feed thy people with thy rod, the flock of thy inheritance, them that dwell alone in the forest, in the midst of Carmel: they shall feed in Basan and Galaad, according to the days of old.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: According to the days of thy coming out of the land of Egypt, I will shew him wonders.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The nations shall see, and shall be confounded at all their strength: they shall put the hand upon the mouth, their ears shall be deaf.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: They shall lick the dust like serpents, as the creeping things of the earth, they shall be disturbed in their houses: they shall dread the Lord, our God, and shall fear thee.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Who is a God like to thee, who takest away iniquity, and passest by the sin of the remnant of thy inheritance? he will send his fury in no more, because he delighteth in mercy.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: He will turn again, and have mercy on us: he will put away our iniquities: and he will cast all our sins into the bottom of the sea.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Thou wilt perform the truth to Jacob, the mercy to Abraham: which thou hast sworn to our fathers from the days of old.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner
